notes: HGSS-era booster (HS—Triumphant, hgss4, Nov 2010): 10 cards/pack = 1 rare-or-better slot + 3 uncommon + 5 common + 1 reverse-holo; box = 36 packs; NO ETB. The rare-or-better slot distribution is built from ThePriceDex's per-slot premium rates for this exact set, cross-validated by PackRip and the Flipside era article: secret-rare 1/216=0.00463 (Flipside: ~1 in 3 boxes / 1:108-216; ThePriceDex 0.2/box); legend 1/14.4=0.06944 (ThePriceDex 2.5 halves/box; era avg 1:12-18); rare-holo 1/3.9=0.25641 (ThePriceDex ~9.3/box). Prime tier: ThePriceDex gives 1/7.2=0.13889 for the Prime slot; PackRip independently states the Prime tier fires 11.10%/pack (8 Primes, Gengar #94 = 1-in-72, ~40% chance per box). I reconciled these by splitting ThePriceDex's 13.89% into prime=0.11100 (matches PackRip exactly) and ultra-rare=0.02789 — the set's single ultra-rare/SP-tier card (bucket ultra-rare:1) sits in this premium band and accounts for the gap between the two sources. Plain rare = residual = 1 − 0.46937 = 0.53063. Slot sums to exactly 1.0. Per-box checks: ~4.0 Primes (community 4-6 ✓), ~2.5 LEGEND halves ✓, ~0.17 secret rare ✓, ~9.2 holos ✓. The legend bucket count is 3 (the set's 2 LEGEND pairs / 4 halves (Darkrai & Cresselia #99-100, Palkia & Dialga #101-102); pokemontcg lists top+bottom). Vintage box price carries a large sealed-collector premium, so EV/booster is expected to sit far below box price — rates were NOT inflated to compensate. confidence=medium: corroborated by ThePriceDex + PackRip + Flipside + PokeBeach community samples, but no large official published study exists for this vintage set.
